macro_summary: SPY edged up 0.2% to 745.64 on Monday as oil prices held steady, while NVIDIA slid 2.18% and AMD jumped 3.3% on a $10B Taiwan AI investment announcement. The US 10-year yield slipped to 4.56%, but hawkish FOMC minutes and persistent Hormuz-driven inflation kept rate-cut expectations at zero. Singapore Q1 GDP grew 6.0% (above advance estimate), lifting local banks, though core inflation pressures persisted.
commodities_note: WTI closed flat at $96.60, down 11.1% on the week amid mixed signals from US‑Iran negotiations. Gold was unchanged at $4,523.20, while the US dollar index eased 0.3% to 99.03. Oil prices remain above the pre‑Hormuz level, but the 5-day slide suggests some de‑escalation hopes are being priced.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain CAUTION on overbought RSI and thin volume, yet the relative divergence persists — OCBC’s NIM resilience continues to differentiate it from DBS and UOB. Singapore’s Q1 GDP surprise (+6.0% y/y) underscores strong domestic activity, but the MAS’s tight S$NEER stance and US10Y above 4.20% sustain the dual NIM compression headwind. No bank is near an entry zone.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM relief stalled, technicals overbought.
singapore.key_development: Singapore Q1 GDP up 6.0% y/y (per Reuters, May 25) but MAS kept policy tight in April; no NIM catalyst until October review.

semiconductors.summary: Extreme dispersion marks the cluster: AMD (+54%), INTC (+58%), SK Hynix (+55%), MU (+45%) are in vertical rallies while AVGO (-42% vs cluster) and TSM (-35% vs cluster) lag. NVDA shed 2.2% and is now underperforming the cluster by 8.1% over the week. No name is within a workable entry zone — all 11 members are hard‑blocked on extension, RSI, or poor risk/reward.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — sector beta unreliable for fresh entry.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD’s $10B Taiwan AI investment (per WSJ, May 21) added fuel to the rally, while TSEM’s $1.3B photonics deals (per Simply Wall St., May 24) extended its breakout.

big_tech.summary: AMZN and GOOG remain CAUTION on extension, while MSFT holds near its 50‑day but with tight upside at $424.43. PLTR’s CAUTION is reinforced by a 9/10 drawdown calibration and a DIA contract challenge. The cluster’s only positive divergence is MSFT’s proximity to an entry trigger, but it needs a break above $424.43 on volume first.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — MSFT near action point, rest of cloud names still extended.
big_tech.key_development: Berkshire Hathaway exited AMZN entirely while building a top‑5 position in GOOG (per Simply Wall St., May 24), highlighting a shift in AI infrastructure bets within BigTech.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking beneficiary — reads through to NOK’s AI optical and NVDA’s Spectrum‑X switch demand as hyperscalers upgrade back‑end fabrics.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET pulls back 8‑10% from current levels and NVDA’s next earnings call confirms data‑center networking acceleration.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA’s $2B photonics investment in LITE has a matching commitment to COHR; a direct read‑through to AI optical interconnect demand as NVDA scales its in‑house transceiver ecosystem.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR corrects 10‑15% from current levels toward its 200‑day moving average, with volume confirmation.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO — Google TPU and Meta training chip shift from merchant GPUs to custom silicon benefits MRVL’s ASIC pipeline. AVGO’s upcoming June 4 earnings will provide the next signal on custom‑silicon market share.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO’s June 4 print; add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back 15‑20% to a major support on a rotation out of megacap semis.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz — US‑Iran peace talks stalled; oil still elevated at $96‑104 with transit fees/insurance disruption, though some de‑escalation hopes are creeping in.
Hawkish Fed — FOMC minutes show majority backing rate hikes if inflation persists; US10Y at 4.56% after a volatile week.
US‑China tariff truce — agriculture tariff cuts agreed, but broad semiconductor export controls unchanged; supply chain uncertainty lingers.
ECB rate hike pressure — Eurozone contraction deepens, yet ECB signals a June hike; divergence widens.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil prices keep energy‑sensitive sectors (Singapore banks, TSMC) under pressure, while defense/AI names (PLTR, AMD) benefit from higher spending. The hawkish Fed anchors yields above 4.5%, capping multiple expansion for extended semis and BigTech. The 5‑day drop in WTI has eased some immediate rate‑hike fears, but inflation expectations remain un‑anchored.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Singapore Q1 GDP grew 6.0% y/y (per Reuters, May 25), beating advance estimate; core inflation expected at 1.7% for April.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries across the board — 14 of 22 watchlist names are hard‑blocked on technical extension; wait for a pullback in semis or a break in the oil‑Fed feedback loop before deploying fresh capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensively positioned — extreme extension dominates, no BUY or ACCUMULATE signals.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 14 of 22 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension; the remaining 8 lack workable risk/reward. The primary risk is that a sudden de‑escalation in Hormuz could trigger a violent rotation out of extended AI/semis names.

Support at $7.23 holding; enter on $7.95 reclaim. ADR risk caps sizing.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
